author: ivan-lednev
description: Move completed tasks to an archive with a date tree.

manifest.author: Ivan Lednev
manifest.description: Move completed tasks to an archive with a date tree
manifest.name: Archiver

name: Archiver

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed issues

- Text replacement now replaces every match instead of just the first one

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New features

- Use patterns in rules to match against task text contents
- Delete tasks that match a rule

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed issues

- Fix plugin installation error

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New features

- You can now archive tasks to daily note (Settings > Archive to separate file > Daily note). If the note doesn't exist, it's going to be created using the daily note template from your daily note settings.

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
## Fixed issues

- Respect custom date format when archiving to a separate file

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
## Fixed issues

- Archiving no longer messes with front-matter
- Archiving no longer messes with indentation in code blocks

## Breaking changes

- Removed support for command: "Turn list items at this level into headings", as it was buggy and didn't fit with the purpose of the plugin.

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
- Now rules can handle unchecked tasks (see #46)
- Fixed bug with bullets without check-boxes blocking archiving of parent tasks (see #47)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Minor bug fixes

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - You can now apply rules to tasks by file paths that match a certain pattern (partially addresses #41, #29)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Fix rewriting old settings (#38)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - There is now a `{{headingChain}}` placeholder that allows you to append all the headings to completed tasks (see #33).

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - There is now an option to archive only those tasks in which there are no incomplete subtasks (see #36)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Added a new placeholder for file names, headings, lists: `{{obsidianTasksCompletedDate}}`

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Small bug fixes

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - You can now archive tasks under arbitrary headings & list items.

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Add toggle to sort tasks alphabetically before archiving

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
- You can now add rules to send tasks with custom statuses to different files:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41428836/223446199-395301b7-2fe2-444d-9894-dcfc5ca972c7.png)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Fix a bug with displaying settigns when no file is open

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Now if you archive tasks to a separate file, you can do so without creating archive headings

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
You can now add the source heading to archived tasks using `{{heading}}` (addresses #33):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41428836/222503107-08ad2e25-3d75-4b21-b934-df8f47383b22.png)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Add `{{sourceFilePath}}` placeholder to configuration. You can use it to archive tasks to a separate file that's in the same directory as the source file (see #34)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Create parent folders for archive file if they don't exist (see #27)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
Add option to sort archived tasks newest first (see #28):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41428836/222133524-7383ff31-7e7c-4673-bc89-1bef55632659.png)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
You can now append text to archived tasks. Placeholders available: `{{date}}`, `{{sourceFileName}}`. This provides better traceability (see #13).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41428836/222103274-d58ac7b2-2f7e-4b4e-bc67-43d4c3e7dc60.png)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
- **Breaking change**: there is a toggle now for using an additional task pattern, so if you use this feature, you'll have to enable it
- Rewrite the settings UI: add test fields for playing around with patterns and replacements
- Add command: "Toggle task done and archive it" (see #30) 
- Add special placeholders to the option `Archive tasks to a separate file`: `{{date}}`, `{{sourceFileName}}`. You can now archive tasks to daily or any periodic notes (see #22)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Prevent having to restart Obsidian or re-load the plug-in after updating the settings for them to take effect.

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Search for archive headings at any level (addresses #12)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Add an option to archive any checked task (`[>]`, `[-]`, etc.)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Add a way to replace text in archived tasks. Check out the settings tab to configure this. This addresses #19.

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Enable filtering by an arbitrary task string. Check out the settings tab to configure the string (thanks, @wealthychef1)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Add command: 'Archive tasks including nested tasks in this file'

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Fix bug with reading indentation settings

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
## Add command: 'Turn list items at this level into headings'

Grab the list under cursor and turn every list item at and above the level of the item under cursor into a heading.

This:

```markdown
- li 1
    - li 2 | <- cursor
        - li 3
```

Turns into:

```markdown
# li 1

## li 2

- li 3
```


## Add command: 'Sort tasks in list under cursor recursively'

Grab the whole list under cursor and **recursively** reorder all the items based on completeness:

1. Plain list items first
2. Then, incomplete tasks
3. And finally, completed tasks

This list:

```markdown
- [x] Task
- Item
- [ ] Incomplete
    - [x] Task
    - Item More notes
    - [ ] Incomplete
- Item 2
- [ ] Incomplete 2
    - [x] Task
    - Item
    - [x] Task 2
```

Turns into:

```markdown
- Item
- Item 2
- [ ] Incomplete
    - Item More notes
    - [ ] Incomplete
    - [x] Task
- [ ] Incomplete 2
    - Item
    - [x] Task
    - [x] Task 2
- [x] Task
```


## Add command: 'Archive heading under cursor'

Grab the whole section under the heading under cursor, including all the child sections and move it to the archive.

This:

```markdown
Some top-level text

# H1 heading

Some text

## H2 heading

More text
```

Turns into:

```markdown
Some top-level text

# Archived

## H1 heading

Some text

### H2 heading

More text
```

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description: - Fix command not in command palette in reading mode (#11)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
- Clean up the code base
- Add command: “Delete tasks in active file” (addresses #4)
- Fix parser breaking when jumping heading levels (addresses #7)
- Fix having to wait for Obsidian to write updated file contents to the disk. Now completed tasks can be archived instantly

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
# New features

- Add the day option to the date tree (addresses https://github.com/ivan-lednev/obsidian-task-archiver/issues/5)
- Add an option for archiving tasks to a separate file (partially addresses https://github.com/ivan-lednev/obsidian-task-archiver/issues/4)
    - You can archive all the completed tasks to a single file, or define a separate archive file per each file like so: `% (archive)`; where `%` will get replaced with the active file name
- Add an option to set the archive heading depth (addresses https://github.com/ivan-lednev/obsidian-task-archiver/issues/2)
- Make newlines around the archive heading optional (addresses https://github.com/ivan-lednev/obsidian-task-archiver/issues/3)

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
Improvements
- Add an archive to the file if there is none
- Escape archive heading text (now `[[internal links]]` can be used as archive headings)
Bug fixes
- Warn the user on trying to run the archiver inside non-markdown files

releases.author: ivan-lednev
releases.description:
- Notify the user about the results of the operation
- Support numbered tasks
- Normalize newlines in the archive
- Use values from the app config when indenting lines instead of always using 4 spaces
- Fix: extract only top-level tasks
